title: Combined schema + API breaking change (field rename propagated)
description: The profiles table renames full_name to display_name at the schema level, and this rename propagates to the GET /profiles/{id} API response. The rename is a two-part breaking change: the schema loses full_name (breaking for any direct DB consumers) and gains display_name (safe addition). The API mirrors this: the response drops full_name (breaking for API consumers) and adds display_name (safe). Clients that deserialize the response into a typed model with a full_name field will fail at runtime.

schema_changes.reason: Removing a NOT NULL column from an existing table breaks any reader or writer that references full_name directly.
Adding a new column (even NOT NULL) is a safe additive change from a schema-diff perspective, though data migration is required.

api_changes.reason: Any API consumer that reads full_name from the response will get a deserialization error or null value after this change.
Adding a new field to a response is backward-compatible for consumers that ignore unknown fields.
